item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unconvincing diary entries that felt shoehorned in and a random interview with someone that also felt shoehorned in.

Also, too much tell and not enough show, even for a diary entry and interview. Good concept, meh execution. Apologies, but -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm generally of two minds on this. Yes, the story is in there somewhere. It might even be a pretty nice story. Unfortunately, it is both too distant and not initially interesting enough to get me invested. I'm not saying every story needs to be fully explored of course. I just didn't feel like this one gave me enough of an initial investment for me to feel gratified in searching for the rest.
That, and a lot of the dialogue feels pretty clunky to me.

Here's an example:

I think she said something, but I took off running, so I don't remember exactly what her exact words, but…

That's a very difficult sentence to read. I know it's spoken dialogue, so it doesn't have to be grammatically perfect, but it helps to be enjoyable to read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love the idea of this one, it’s cute +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was thinking the journal wasn't actually necessary, then it turned into "the damn trees are here" and I was hooked again.

That said, the interview's not super great? I mean, the interviewer calling them 'hugging trees' right out the bat, and then they're like, omg, the trees hugged her what the shit when obviously, we know that they know what's going on here.

Still gets a +1 from me, 2 outta 3 ain't bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved the read, would've been better if we found more about Jade, but a +1 from me non the lesss.

item.title: Re: a few typos here and there but great story overall +1
Re: a few typos here and there but great story overall +1
item.content:encoded: Just to note, amnestized was correct, from what I can tell. I'm assuming Dr Djocovk doesn't know that amnestics are a thing, and amnestied doesn't make a whole lot of sense in this context.

item.title: Re: a few typos here and there but great story overall +1
a few typos here and there but great story overall +1
item.content:encoded:
Typos and or grammatical errors will be put in »these things«. Great story though, just trying to make it a little better haha

Witnesses of SCP-5481 are to be interrogated and »amnesticized.«

Amnestied**

I don't even know where I am going to live now. I…For once I »though« I could live somewhere safe. At least for some time. But no. Once again, you RUINED it. Thank you, mom.

Thought**

Mr. Walters: … no. I ran to my house and told my wife about it. She »though« someone had drugged me, so she called the cops.

Thought**

+ show block
– hide block

The journal of a presumed female known as "Jade Johnsson", now designated PoI-5481, was found in the surroundings of the »cabin,« and is transcribed below.

No need for the comma after "cabin".

We were a family! Every decision affected »everyone« of us!

The word is used incorrectly and should be spelled "every one" instead of "everyone", however, this is one of the "notes" so I think it can be let off for spelling errors, especially since he seems to be writing this in an angered state, it would be ok for his grammar and spelling and such to be much poorer.

Mr. Walters: Yeah, I guess. I was at the park. It was, like nine or ten. I was gonna smoke a »cigarette,« when I discovered that there was someone in the swings.

No need for a comma after "cigarette".

Mr. Walters: Yeah, I know it »sound« crazy, but that damn tree- two arms came out of it and hugged her! I'm not crazy! I know what» i« saw! I-

Sounds** and capitalize "i".

Dr. Hammil: Mr. Walters, please calm down. Nobody's doubting your testimony. What »happen afterwards«?

You could say "What happened afterward?" as saying "happen afterwards" just doesn't sound right in my opinion.

Ok yeah, I'm sorry for this being so long I just wanted to point these things out and take these typos out.


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The story about an overbearing mother trying to make things with love but turns out she's just making worst is a cool and nice twist. But the notes just doesn't do it for me when trying to explore this story. I wanted a little more delving into that concept and not just an interview of doing describing the PoI sadness.

item.title: Re: I have an SCP! :D
item.content:encoded: You have a typo in the containment procedures with the word instance, with instead of having one n you have two n’s.

item.title: I have an SCP! :D
item.content:encoded:
The hardest part of writing an SCP is the formatting.

-Me.

Wow! Finally, after, like, 4 years or so, I've got an SCP! :D

I still remember those days when I was 14 and I just read skips for fun. Wow, those days seem very far away, I'm fricking old.

Big thanks to all who helped me, which may include, but is not limited to: Cerastes, DrDromeus, Blightknell and whoever read my draft in #thecritters!

And, of course, big-spectacular-extraordinary-awesome-fantastic-gigatic thanks to @devilman_25 for the drawings! They might be simple to some people, but they help A LOT to those who don't know how to draw! (me).

Please let me know your thoughts about it!
